resposta:
solution: ```class Solution:
    def largestMerge(self, word1: str, word2: str) -> str:
        i = j = 0
        m, n = len(word1), len(word2)
        res = []
        while i < m and j < n:
            if word1[i:] > word2[j:]:
                res.append(word1[i])
                i += 1
            else:
                res.append(word2[j])
                j += 1
        res.extend(word1[i:])
        res.extend(word2[j:])
        return ''.join(res)
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O((m+n)^2)
space complexity: O(m+n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: Greedy choice at each step: always pick the prefix that is lexicographically larger. Suffix comparison is O(k) per step but bounded by string lengths; Python slicing is optimized.
